entry.title: Data Templates for DDS: Expense Administration SAC Story
entry.content:
Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service Expense

SAP Travel & Expense: Expense Administration SAC Story

This story offers a comprehensive analysis of expenditure, outstanding spend, and workflow efficiency (based on SAP Datasphere). By integrating and visualizing spending data alongside outstanding balances, it provides insights into financial transactions and workflow performance. 

The Concur Travel & Expense Data Templates for the Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) Expense content package provides a powerful solution for visualizing and analyzing data captured in SAP Concur using SAP Analytics Cloud, facilitated by a seamless integration through SAP Data Delivery Services. With this solution, administrators can gain comprehensive insights into organizational expenses, system administration and user distributions. The advanced analytics capabilities extend to robust auditing and control features, which are critical for identifying potential fraud and ensuring compliance. Built upon Analytical Models in SAP Datasphere, this content package includes a suite of detailed stories that offer in-depth perspectives on expense management. These insights enable administrators to make informed decisions, optimize expense workflows, and enhance overall financial governance. 

Spend Analysis Page 

This dashboard offers a comprehensive overview of your company's financial landscape by presenting key metrics related to combined spend from expense reports. It serves as a centralized hub for monitoring and analyzing financial performance, aiding in strategic decision-making and budget management.  

  • Trend Analysis: Allowing to analyze trends for the Top 3 Expense Types Over Time (based on Sent for Payment Date)  
  • Top 10 Expense Type: Presenting a breakdown of Expenses per Expense Type.  
  • Top Payment Types: Revealing key insights into payment preferences.  
  • Total Spend per Vendor: Providing top vendors analysis.  
  • Top 10 Spending Employees: identifying highest spenders within a company.   
  • Spend Details: Providing a comprehensive breakdown of expenses, categorized by Spend Category and accompanied by respective expense claims and claiming employees.  
  • Associated Key Performance Indicators: 1) Total Spend: displaying Total Approved Amount according to a selected time period. 2) # Processed reports: displaying the number of reports that were approved and sent for payment.  

Outstanding Spend

This dashboard offers a comprehensive view of pending expenses across your organization. It provides key metrics related to outstanding expenses yet to be reimbursed or processed, allowing to monitor and managing accruals. By highlighting pending expenses and tracking their status, this dashboard aids in optimizing expenses and ensuring timely reimbursement, thereby contributing to better financial and resource management.  

  • Trend Analysis: This chart offers a comprehensive analysis of trends in outstanding transactions over time.  
  • Aging Transactions: This chart displays the distribution of claims based on their associated aging in days, aiding in prioritizing and monitoring resolution and approval/processing efforts.  
  • Top Unprocessed Expense Types: This chart offers a detailed breakdown of outstanding spend per expense type, highlighting the top expense categories awaiting processing.   
  • Outstanding Expense per Merchant: This chart presents a breakdown of outstanding spend across various vendors, providing insights into top vendors for pending expenses.  
  • Top 10 Employees with Unprocessed Expense: This chart identifies the top employees with outstanding expenses, providing insights into individual spending patterns and possibility aiding in timely reimbursement and expense management.  
  • Outstanding Spend – Transactional Details: This detailed table provides a comprehensive breakdown of accrual spend, showcasing pending expenses along with its associated details namely, spend category, merchant information, employee details etc.  

Workflow Analysis

This dashboard provides an in-depth overview of your company's operational processes by presenting key metrics related to workflow efficiency and performance. It serves as a centralized hub for monitoring and analyzing the productivity and effectiveness of various workflows, aiding in process optimization and strategic decision-making.

  • Workflow Cycle: This chart provides a detailed analysis of the median number of days required to complete each step in the workflow process. 
  • Distribution of Aging Reports: This chart displays the number of reports categorized by their associated aging time, providing insights into the duration of pending reports. 
  • Claims Ever Sent Back to Employees: This chart highlights the number of claims that have been returned to employees for further action or clarification. 
  • Expense Reports within Policy: This chart illustrates the number of expense reports that comply with the company's policy guidelines, ensuring adherence to established standards. 
  • Workflow Summary: This chart provides an overview of the entire workflow process, summarizing key metrics and performance indicators to offer a holistic view of operational efficiency. 
  • Expense Reports Approval – Details: This chart provides a detailed breakdown of the expense report approval process, including key metrics on approval times, approval rates, and any pending approvals.

entry.title: SPM Embedded Analytics Insights with SAC Publications
entry.content:
As organizations continue to leverage data to drive business decisions, the ability to efficiently share insights and reports becomes increasingly crucial. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) offers a powerful feature known as Publications, which allows users to automate the distribution of stories and dashboards to a broad audience. In this blog, we’ll dive into the SAC Publication feature, explore key areas of scheduling, and highlight its pivotal role in streamlining operations.

What is SAC Publication?

The SAC Publication feature enables users to schedule and distribute stories and dashboards to designated recipients automatically. Whether you need to share daily sales reports, monthly performance metrics, or ad-hoc analyses, SAC Publications ensures that the right people receive the right information at the right time.

The Role of Publications in Embedded Analytics (EA)

The Publication feature plays a pivotal role within Embedded Analytics (EA), particularly for Compensation Administrators. It allows them to schedule the generation and delivery of both Published Reports and Personalized Reports to end users.

  • Published Reports: These are standardized reports generated by EA that provide consistent insights across the organization.
  • Personalized Reports: These reports are customized to cater to individual preferences, needs, or specific requirements of users.

The system enables Compensation Administrators to automate the report generation process, ensuring timely and tailored delivery of crucial information directly to users' inboxes. This capability streamlines operations, granting administrators precise control over report distribution and enhancing the overall efficiency of the reporting process.

Key Features of SAC Publication

  1. Automated Scheduling:
    • Time-Saving Automation: Instead of manually exporting and sending reports, SAC allows you to automate this process. Reports can be scheduled to run at specific times, ensuring timely delivery.
    • Frequency Options: Users can choose from a variety of scheduling options, such as daily, weekly, monthly, or even custom intervals, depending on the needs of the organization.
  2. Format Flexibility:
    • Multiple Formats: SAC supports various output formats, including PDF, Microsoft Excel, and PowerPoint. This flexibility allows users to tailor the report format to the preferences of the recipients.
    • Personalized Content: SAC Publications can be customized to include specific filters or views, ensuring that each recipient receives relevant data.
  3. Secure Distribution:
    • Access Control: Publications are distributed securely, respecting the data access rights and permissions of each recipient. This ensures that sensitive information is only shared with authorized users.
    • Recipient Management: You can easily manage and update the list of recipients, including individuals and groups, within SAC.
  4. Error Handling and Notifications:
    • Error Alerts: If a scheduled publication encounters issues, SAC can notify administrators or designated users, allowing for quick resolution.
    • Success Notifications: Recipients can also receive notifications when a new report is available, ensuring they are aware of the latest insights.

Key Areas of Scheduling in SAC

Scheduling plays a critical role in the effective use of SAC Publications. Here are some key areas to consider:

  1. Defining the Schedule:
    • Timing: Consider the optimal time for report distribution. For example, scheduling a report to be sent at the start of the business day ensures that decision-makers have the latest data available when they begin work.
    • Frequency: Determine how often the data needs to be refreshed and reported. High-frequency scheduling (e.g., daily) may be necessary for dynamic, fast-paced environments, while less frequent updates may suffice for static or long-term analyses.
  2. Selecting the Right Audience:
    • Targeted Distribution: Identify the key stakeholders who need access to the reports. By segmenting your audience, you can ensure that each group receives information that is most relevant to their roles.
    • Role-Based Access: Use SAC’s role-based access controls to ensure that only authorized users receive sensitive data, maintaining compliance with data governance policies.
  3. Managing Report Formats:
    • Standardization: Standardizing the report format across the organization can help in maintaining consistency and ease of interpretation. Decide on the most suitable format (PDF, Excel, etc.) based on the needs of your audience.
    • Customization: While standardization is important, consider offering customized views or filtered data for specific recipients, especially if they require a focused analysis.
  4. Monitoring and Optimization:
    • Reviewing Schedules: Regularly review and adjust publication schedules to align with changing business needs or recipient feedback.
    • Performance Monitoring: Monitor the performance of scheduled publications to ensure they are delivered without delays or errors. SAC’s built-in monitoring tools can help identify bottlenecks or issues.

Step-by-Step Guide: How to Schedule a Publication in SAC

Automating the report generation process involves scheduling jobs to run at predefined intervals, ensuring regular and systematic generation of reports without manual intervention. Here’s how you can schedule a publication:

  1. Login to Embedded Analytics.
  2. Navigate to the Public folder.
  3. Select a Story and open it.
  4. Click on File -> Schedule Publication.
     
     

    • The Schedule Publication window will open. Enter the details Event Name, Start Date, Destination, Email Subject, Internal Users and Select File Type and click Create.

 

    • Upon successful creation, navigate to Calendar to view the scheduled publication.

    • Open the publication to review details and settings.
  1. Verify the Publication:
    • Once the publication is successful, verify that the generated PDF report is delivered to the user’s inbox.

    • Open the user folder and check the generated report to ensure accuracy and completeness.

Best Practices for SAC Publications

  • Test Before Deploying: Always test your publication settings with a small group before rolling them out organization-wide. This helps identify any potential issues with format, content, or timing.
  • Document Your Schedule: Maintain documentation of your publication schedules and configurations. This will be useful for troubleshooting and ensuring continuity in case of staff changes.
  • Regularly Update Recipients: Keep your recipient lists up-to-date to ensure that reports are sent to the correct individuals. This is especially important in large organizations with frequent personnel changes.

Conclusion

The SAC Publication feature is a powerful tool for automating the distribution of reports and dashboards, saving time and ensuring consistency across the organization. By understanding the key areas of scheduling and following best practices, you can maximize the efficiency and effectiveness of your reporting processes in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Explore SAC Publications today, and take your reporting strategy to the next level!

This blog provides a comprehensive overview of the SAC Publication feature, emphasizing its importance in Embedded Analytics and offering actionable insights for effective scheduling and automation.

entry.title: Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) : CREDIT CARD PROGRAM MANAGEMENT SAC Story
entry.content:
Overview

The Concur Expense Data Templates for the Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) Expense content package offers a powerful solution for visualizing and analyzing data captured in SAP Concur. Seamlessly integrated with SAP Data Delivery Services and utilizing the SAP Analytics Cloud for data visualization, this solution provides administrators with comprehensive insights into organizational expenses, system administration, and user distributions. Its advanced analytics capabilities include robust auditing and control features essential for fraud detection and compliance assurance.

Built upon Analytical Models in SAP Datasphere and visualized in SAP Analytics Cloud, this content package encompasses a suite of detailed stories that deliver in-depth perspectives on expense management. These insights empower administrators to make informed decisions, optimize expense workflows, and enhance overall financial governance.

One standout feature is the Credit Card Program Management SAC Story. Designed to streamline and oversee credit card programs, it integrates and visualizes card usage data to provide a holistic view of cardholder activity and program efficiency. Additionally, it offers detailed insights into credit card transactions, highlighting spending patterns, transaction volumes, and potential anomalies, thereby significantly enhancing financial oversight and fraud detection.

Credit Card Program Management

This dashboard offers a comprehensive overview of your company's financial landscape by presenting key metrics related to credit card spend. It serves as a centralized hub for monitoring and analyzing credit card related financial performance, aiding in strategic decision-making and budget management.  

  • Adoption Rate: Presenting the proportion of credit card usage in total transactions (entries) 
  • Adoption Rate by Employee Location: Analysis of the geographic spread of credit card adoption among employees 
  • Top Spenders by Payment Type: Shows a breakdown of highest credit card spend across Payment Types 
  • Top Spenders by Payment Type: Provides key insights into highest credit card spenders and their payment preferences. 
  • Top Credit Card Spend by Vendor: Displays the vendors with the highest credit card expenditure.  

 

Credit Card Transaction Analysis

This dashboard offers an analytical view of credit card spend across your organization. It provides key metrics related to credit card expenses yet to be processed, allowing to monitor and manage unprocessed spend. By highlighting the distribution of transactions across age brackets or per approval status this dashboard aids tracking credit card spend across time or also allows a view via the regional distribution, thus allowing a complete overview for making decisions.  

  • Aging Card Transactions: This chart shows the distribution of Card Transactions across different age brackets.  
  • Spend by Country: Provides a breakdown of credit card expenditure across countries.  
  • Credit Card Transaction per Approval Status: Displays an analysis of transactions by approval status.  
  • Top Employees with Unprocessed CC Transactions: Presents key employees with outstanding credit card transactions.  
  • Credit Card Transaction Details: Displays a detailed list of employee credit card spend.  

Resources:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-dds-expense-administration-sac-story/ba-p/13805542

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-expense/ba-p/13799924

  • https://www.concur.com/data-delivery-service

  • https://api.sap.com/package/SAPConcurExpenseDDSIntegrationwithSAPDatasphere/overview

entry.title: ​​​​SAP Datasphere and Collibra: End-to-End Data Catalog Integration ​​​
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that the integration between SAP Datasphere and Collibra Data Intelligence Platform is now generally available. This integration enables an end-to-end data ​​catalog across SAP and non-SAP sources for a business data fabric, allowing customers to do more with trusted data.​​ 

​​​This blog describes the integration and how it enables metadata to be exchanged between SAP Datasphere catalog and Collibra Data Catalog. The integration facilitates efficient metadata exchange, ensuring robust data governance across your entire data ecosystem with SAP and non-SAP data.​​ 

​​​Configuring SAP Datasphere catalog​​ 

​First let’s have a look at how we configure SAP Datasphere to expose the published assets in the Catalog so it can be consumed in the Collibra Data Catalog.​​​​

Within SAP Datasphere, we have access to our comprehensive catalog. This catalog is our gateway to various assets that are readily available. We can start right from the home page, navigate to the catalog, and directly access all the available SAP metadata that were harvested and enriched. ​

In ​​​the SAP Datasphere ​catalog, we find assets from different connected systems, including terms, KPIs, and other asset types. These assets come from a variety of connected systems from which we harvest the metadata.​

We can navigate to the monitoring associated to the catalog to​ see a list of systems we​ are able to connect and harvest the metadata from. This includes creating new connections to SAP systems such as SAP Analytics Cloud​​​, ​​​SAP Datasphere​​​, or SAP Datasphere BW bridge​. 

To ensure the metadata in SAP Datasphere can be consumed in Collibra, we need to create an OAuth client ​application ​integration​ entry point​. ​​​ 

​​​To create a new OAuth client, access System, then App Integration and click “Add a New OAuth Client”.​​

We can create a new OAuth client, which will allow us to expose the various types of metadata available in our system to Collibra through APIs. ​​​ ​​

The OAuth Client requires a Name, the Purpose must be “API Access” with a “Catalog User API” Access.​​ 

​​When the OAuth client is created, it will generate an ​OAuth ​Client I​D​​​ and a secret that we can use​ in Collibra ​​as an​​​​ entry point to access the available APIs.​​​​​​ ​​

Importing SAP Analytics Cloud Metadata into Collibra Data Catalog​​ 

​​With the OAuth Client I​D​​​ and secret in hand, we can now switch to Collibra to define the entry point to the ​SAP Datasphere catalog​​​. ​​​ ​​

In Collibra, we go to ​​Edge and create a connection using the OAuth details.​​​ ​

​We set up an SAP Datasphere catalog connection by entering the name, description, and the URL of the Datasphere tenant catalog. Then, we use the OAuth Client I​D​​​ and secret generated earlier.​​​ ​​

Once these details are filled in, the connection between Collibra and SAP Datasphere will be established.​​​​​​ 

​​Now it's time to create capabilities​ such as retrieval of​​​ metadata related to SAP Analytics Cloud assets, or different capabilities for other SAP Datasphere asset types. ​​​ ​​

This flexibility allows ​retrieval of​​​ metadata per asset type, tailoring it to your needs.​​​​​

Once a capability is created, we simply hit refresh​​​ to pull the metadata from SAP Datasphere into Collibra, making them accessible in the Collibra catalog.​​​​​​ ​​

​​​Once synchronized, all the metadata are available now in the Collibra Data Catalog to consume.​​ 

Checking available Metadata in Collibra Data Catalog​​ 

​​We can now ​check ​​that​​​​ the metadata ​​were retrieved from SAP Datasphere and ​verify ​​​that all information is available ​by searching​,​ for ​example​​,​​ ​a specific SAP Analytics Cloud model in SAP Datasphere. ​​​ ​​

Let’s search for a particular model and then let’s ​​​examine all the information for this asset​​​.​ ​​​ ​

When we look at the lineage, we can see how the model is used by different SAP Analytics Cloud stories. One might be in the public folder while another is in the system folder. ​

We can now switch to Collibra to confirm that the same information for this asset is now available in the system. ​​​​​​ ​​

We search for the asset by its name and ​​access the detailed metadata including​​​​ the lineage graph, which will display the same information​​ as the one that ​​was​​​​ available in the SAP Datasphere catalog​​.​​​​ ​​

In this lineage graph, we retrieve the information about the model we searched​​ for, and we also see that the model is used by the different SAP Analytics Cloud stories that are in the public folder and in the system folder.​​ ​​

Additionally, Collibra was able to retrieve the available column details for the SAP Analytics Cloud model asset and integrate these details directly in the lineage graph ​​allowing end​​​ ​users a better and more detailed view of the object in the lineage graph.​​​​ 

In summary, we've shown how SAP ​delivers robust​​​ data governance with Collibra and how SAP Datasphere empowers you to harness the full potential of your business data to derive critical business ​decisions​​​.​ The integration between the SAP Datasphere catalog and the Collibra ​​D​​​​ata ​​C​​​​atalog streamlines data management, enabling users to efficiently oversee the entire data lifecycle across both platforms​​,​​ helping to increase data governance within organizations.​ 

​​​For an in​​-​​​​product tour of this functionality go to the Collibra Data Intelligence​​ Platform with SAP Datasphere​​ ​​product tour available here. ​     ​​​​​​​​​

entry.title: SAC Report Broadcasting - Blog 1 - Imported Data
entry.content:
Introduction

Hello and welcome to this blog series where I will be discussing the functionality of report broadcasting in SAC. I will address the following points:

  • Setting up
  • How to create publications
  • View and Modify your Scheduled Publications
  • SAP and non-SAP Users
  • Data Validation and Refresh

 

Setting up

The first step you need to take to set up SAC report broadcasting is allowing the tenant to schedule a publication. You can set up the tenant so that SAC can publish to either SAP users or non-SAP users or both.

 

  1. Login to your SAC tenant
  2. Go to System >> Administration. Click on the System Configuration tab and toggle the two schedule publication options on.

 

 

How to create publications

  1. Go to your files in SAC
  2. Select the story or analytical application you want to schedule for publication. Choose the Share option and select Schedule Publication.

  1. The Schedule Publication window will open. Here you will add your publication information such as event name, start date etc.

Name

Give your Schedule a name

Start Date

Give your schedule a start date and time. You can add a recurrence so that the publication is scheduled to recur every day/ week/ month etc. You can also add details about ending the recurrence.

File Type

You can choose between either a PDF, PowerPoint or CSV

Email Subject

This is the subject for the email which would be delivered to the recipients

Email Message

This is the message body for the email which would be sent to the recipients over email

Email Delivery Options

You can choose which email delivery option you’d like:

·       Attachment

·       Attachment and Preview Inline

·       Preview Inline

Include link to story

If you select this checkbox, the story/ analytic application link would be sent along with the email.

 

 

  1. Add details to the Distribution heading

SAP Analytics Cloud Recipients

Add SAP users or teams to this view

Non-SAP Analytics Cloud Recipients

Add non-SAP users to this view e.g. joe.bloggs@hotmail.com . You can input a maximum of 3 non-SAC users.

Story view

You can choose the story, global bookmarks or my bookmarks.

File Name

The name the file will appear under in your email as a attachment

 

 

  1. Click on Create. This will set up a schedule a publication.

 

View and Modify your Scheduled Publications

  1. Go to the Calendar app in SAC

 

  1. You can see in Calendar, your scheduled publication events and its recurrence.

  1. You can also edit you’re a recurrence event. Select the recurrence event from the calendar view and on your right side, a new panel opens where you can modify.

 

SAP and Non-SAP Users

You can schedule a publication to be sent to both SAP users and non-SAP users. SAP users are users who have been given a user license for their SAC tenant. They are users who can login to their tenant and view/ create model, stories etc.

Non-SAP users are users who do not have access to the SAC tenant. These email addresses would be considered personal addresses rather than work addresses. You can have a maximum of 3 non-SAP users assigned to a scheduled publication.

 

Data Validation and Refresh

It is important for the data in the scheduled publications are up to date an accurate especially if they are recurring. A user does not want to receive the wrong data or the same data that they received in last week’s report.

Data imported from a CSV file:

If data imported from a CSV file is used to create a story/ analytical application dashboard in SAC, this data will need to be updated manually.

 

Conclusion

In summary, SAC report broadcasting offers a powerful tool for automating your data distribution. By carefully setting up your tenant and creating well-defined publication schedules, allows you to efficiently manage and deliver relevant reports to both SAP and non-SAP users. This capability not only enhances your reporting efficiency but also ensures that stakeholders receive timely and accurate information, ultimately supporting better decision-making and communication within your organisation.
